items.hook: Coast to Coast is 2003 American-Canadian made-for-television drama film starring Richard Dreyfuss, Judy Davis, and Selma Blair, and directed by Paul Mazursky.
items.body: It is based on the 1998 novel by Frederic Raphael, who also wrote film's screenplay. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Dink review – Jake Johnson’s pickleball comedy serves up engagingly silly laughs
items.body:
Johnson is delusional ex-tennis player The Hammer, who has to switch to the sport he loathes after injury

There’s some funny stuff here in this engagingly silly sports comedy from producer-star Jake Johnson about the world of pickl

items.body:
Ray Exworth spent his life filling Cornish sheds with breathtaking sculptures – but wouldn’t let anyone in to view them. Now, after his death, his secret body of work has been revealed

items.body:
The actor emotes his way through Vesuvius’s catastrophic eruption in a docudrama that is embarrassing and exhausting – but undeniably entertaining. Prepare to be Hiddlestoned!

items.hook: ‘We didn’t care what the client wanted!’: Sheffield’s Designers Republic on their art for Aphex Twin, Pulp … and Pringles
items.body:
Their eye-popping designs burst out of Sheffield in 1986, making them the toast of the music scene. Founder Ian Anderson reveals why some fans think his art is worth remortgaging the house for

items.hook: Acts 25 is the twenty-fifth chapter of the Acts of the Apostles in the New Testament of the Christian Bible.
items.body: It records the period of Paul's imprisonment in Caesarea. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Following in the tradition of other Magic blocks, Ravnica takes place in a plane of the multiverse that was previously unexplored in the game's backstory.
items.body: Ravnica is a Magic: The Gathering block that consists of three expert-level expansion sets: Ravnica: City of Guilds, Guildpact, and Dissension. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Carnival of Crime is a 1962 mystery film directed by George Cahan and starring Jean-Pierre Aumont, Alix Talton and Tônia Carrero.
items.body: It is based on the 1956 novel The Sleeping Partner by British writer Winston Graham. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Test of Honor is a 1919 American silent drama film produced by Famous Players–Lasky, released by Paramount, directed by John S.
items.body: Robertson, and starring John Barrymore. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Fires review – volcanoes and passions erupt in Iceland disaster-romance
items.body:
A volcanologist and a photographer try to save Reykjavik in impressive and plausible drama

This scientifically plausible, CGI-tastic disaster film will either do wonders for Iceland’s tourist industry or destroy it completely. Vigd

items.hook: The York County, Maine, Tercentenary half dollar is a 50-cent commemorative coin minted in 1936 to mark the tercentenary of the founding of York County, Maine.
items.body: The obverse shows Brown's Garrison, the fort around which York County was formed, while the reverse depicts the county's arms. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: It was formerly the summer residence and hunting lodge of the Bourbon kings of the Two Sicilies, one of the two royal palaces in Naples.
items.body: The Royal Palace of Capodimonte is a large palazzo in Naples, Italy. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Brunello: The Gracious Visionary review – fawning portrait of the king of cashmere
items.body:
Giuseppe Tornatore traces Italian fashion designer Brunello Cucinelli’s life from poor farming family to billion-dollar empire in a sentimental vanity project

items.hook: Big Bang is a 2019 novel by American writer David Bowman, published almost seven years after his death.
items.body: The introduction is by Jonathan Lethem and was excerpted in The New Yorker. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Wishing Horse of Oz (1935) is the twenty-ninth book in the Oz series created by L.
items.body: Frank Baum and his successors, and the fifteenth written by Ruth Plumly Thompson. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: It is 23 kilometres north-west of the Sydney central business district in the local government area of the City of Parramatta.
